ROUGHLY 15 TREES REPORTED DOWN ON MILL POND RD. IN CHESTERFIELD. DELAYED REPORT.

A VERY BRIEF BUT DAMAGING TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN CHESTERFIELD, NH MONDAY NIGHT AS A THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. EF-0 DAMAGE WAS INITIALLY OBSERVED ON MILL POND RD BEFORE THE TORNADO BECAME STRONGER AND CAUSED SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE SUPPORTING EF-1 CLASSIFICATION JUST NORTH OF MILL POND. THE TORNADO BEGAN TO LIFT SHORTLY AFTER THE EF-1 DAMAGE WITH SPORADIC EF-0 DAMAGE BEFORE QUICKLY ENDING. THE TORNADO DAMAGED APPROXIMATELY 200 TREES WITH A FEW OUTBUILDINGS BEING DESTROYED ON THE PROPERTY. TREE DAMAGE INCLUDED UPROOTED HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS, ALONG WITH SNAPPED TREES. FORTUNATELY NO PERMANENT RESIDENTUAL STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS REPORTED BESIDES DEBRIS CLEAN UP.
